description: Complete iw command and workflow reference from basic to advanced wireless interface management and inspection

commands.explanation: Displays the summary of all available iw commands and usage syntax
Prints the installed version of the iw tool
Lists all physical wireless devices and details their hardware capabilities
Lists all logical wireless interfaces currently recognized by the kernel
Lists all physical wireless hardware (WPHY) present on the system
Shows detailed configuration information for a specific wireless interface
Displays current link status including connected SSID, frequency, and signal strength
Initiates a standard active scan for available wireless networks
Performs a passive scan without sending probe requests to remain stealthy
Scans for networks and prints results with human-readable formatting
Scans only a specific frequency to save time and reduce noise
Initiates an active directed scan specifically for a target SSID
Displays the results of the most recent scan buffer without scanning again
Triggers a background scan without immediately waiting for or printing results

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set type managed
commands.explanation: Sets the interface to managed mode for standard client station operations

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set type monitor
commands.explanation: Sets the interface to monitor mode for packet sniffing and traffic analysis

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set type ibss
commands.explanation: Sets the interface to Ad-Hoc (IBSS) mode for peer-to-peer networking

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set type ap
commands.explanation: Sets the interface to Access Point mode to broadcast a network

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set type wds
commands.explanation: Sets the interface to Wireless Distribution System mode for bridging

commands.example: iw phy phy0 interface add mon0 type monitor
commands.explanation: Creates a new virtual interface on the specified physical hardware
Deletes a specific virtual wireless interface from the system

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set name wifi0
commands.explanation: Renames a wireless interface to a new custom identifier
Forces the interface to listen or transmit on a specific frequency in MHz
Sets the interface to a specific channel number on the 2.4GHz band
Sets frequency with a specific channel width for high throughput testing

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set freq 2412 HT20
commands.explanation: Sets the frequency enforcing a standard 20MHz High Throughput width

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set freq 2437 HT40+
commands.explanation: Sets the frequency enforcing 40MHz width with the secondary channel above

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set freq 2437 HT40-
commands.explanation: Sets the frequency enforcing 40MHz width with the secondary channel below

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set txpower fixed 2000
commands.explanation: Sets a fixed transmit power in millibels (2000 mBm = 20 dBm)

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set txpower auto
commands.explanation: Allows the hardware to automatically manage transmit power levels

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set txpower limit 1000
commands.explanation: Sets a maximum upper limit for transmit power output
Displays the current regulatory domain and frequency constraints
Sets the regulatory domain to a specific country code like US or JP
Attempts to connect to an open wireless network with the specified SSID
Connects to a specific SSID on a forced frequency channel
Connects to a legacy WEP network using a specific key index
Disconnects the interface from the current Access Point
Initiates authentication sequence without full association
Dumps statistics for all currently connected stations or peers
Retrieves detailed signal and throughput stats for a specific station MAC
Forcibly removes a station from the driver's internal table
Checks the current power save mode status of the interface

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set power_save on
commands.explanation: Enables power saving mode to conserve energy during inactivity

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set power_save off
commands.explanation: Disables power saving to ensure maximum performance and consistent Rx/Tx

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set bitrates legacy-2.4 1 2 5.5 11
commands.explanation: Forces the interface to use specific legacy 802.11b bitrates

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set bitrates ht-mcs-2.4 0 1 2 3
commands.explanation: Restricts the interface to use specific HT MCS indexes on 2.4GHz

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set monitor none
commands.explanation: Disables all specific monitor mode flags for a raw capture state

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set monitor fcsfail
commands.explanation: Configures monitor mode to pass packets with bad checksums (FCS errors)

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set monitor control
commands.explanation: Configures monitor mode to capture 802.11 control frames like RTS/CTS

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set monitor otherbss
commands.explanation: Configures monitor mode to capture traffic from other BSSIDs

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set monitor cook
commands.explanation: Enables cooked monitor mode where the hardware handles some processing
Monitors and displays wireless kernel events in real-time
Monitors wireless events and prepends a timestamp to each line
Monitors wireless events and prints full details for each event
Joins an 802.11s mesh network with the specified ID
Leaves the currently joined mesh network
Dumps the current mesh path routing table
Joins or creates an IBSS Ad-Hoc network on a specific frequency
Leaves the current IBSS Ad-Hoc network
Enables 4-address mode usually required for WDS bridging or virtualization
Disables 4-address mode returning to standard 3-address addressing
Sets the coverage class distance in meters to adjust ACK timeouts
Moves the entire physical wireless card into a different network namespace
Sets the RTS threshold to control collision avoidance for packet sizes
Sets the fragmentation threshold to split large packets
Sends a vendor-specific command and receives binary output data
Sets the NoACK map to disable acknowledgments for specific traffic classes

commands.command: iw phy <phyname> interface add mon0 type monitor flags none
commands.example: iw phy phy0 interface add mon0 type monitor flags none
commands.explanation: Creates a clean monitor interface with no filtering flags for full capture

commands.example: iw dev mon0 scan passive dump
commands.explanation: Dumps scan results collected passively to avoid revealing presence
Locks monitor interface to channel 1 with 20MHz width for targeted sniffing

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 scan trigger freq 2412
commands.explanation: Triggers a probe request flood on a single frequency by scanning repeatedly
Periodically dumps station info to detect rogue devices or unauthorized connections
Verifies that the defensive sensor is connected to the correct secure SSID
Audits supported ciphers and interface combinations to ensure hardware compliance
Checks if the regulatory domain has been tampered with or misconfigured
Inspects Wake-on-WLAN capabilities to test power management attacks
Audits mesh parameters to validate routing security settings
Dumps channel survey data to analyze noise floor and channel interference

commands.example: iw dev mon0 set type managed
commands.explanation: Reverts monitor interface to managed to test defense recovery time
Removes temporary monitor interfaces created during the testing session

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set txpower auto
commands.explanation: Resets transmit power to default hardware control after testing
Resets regulatory domain to the world regulatory domain
Shows detailed frequency and channel mapping for the hardware
Displays exhaustive capabilities including VHT/HE support and antenna chains
Sets complex channel width with center frequencies for VHT AP setup
Dumps internal interface state for debugging driver issues

commands.command: iw dev <devname> set type monitor flags fcsfail
commands.example: iw dev wlan0 set type monitor flags fcsfail
commands.explanation: Sets monitor mode and allows bad FCS frames to improve injection analysis
Remains on channel (ROC) for a specific duration in milliseconds
Configures active TX and RX antennas using bitmaps
Configures Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) limitations
Sets the specific power save scheme (1=min, 2=max)

commands.example: iw dev wlan0 measurement request beacon
commands.explanation: Requests 802.11k radio measurement reports from peers
Triggers Fast Transition (802.11r) authentication
Assigns a VLAN ID to the wireless interface packets
Aborts an ongoing scan operation immediately
Dumps information about mesh peers
Sets the hardware retry limit for short and long packets
Sets the temperature threshold for the wireless card

commands.command: iw phy <phyname> interface add <name> type managed
commands.example: iw phy phy0 interface add wlan0 type managed
commands.explanation: Restores a standard managed interface after deleting a monitor interface
Final disconnect command to ensure clean state at end of lab
